news.headline: Brookline Capital Markets Initiates Coverage of Perimeter at Buy, C$1.54 Price Target
news.summary: Brookline Capital Markets analyst Kemp Doliver initiated coverage of Perimeter with a Buy rating and a C$1.54 price target, citing Claire OCT's FDA Breakthrough Device status, which should speed CMS review of the company's planned New Technology APC filing (decision expected by year-end 2026). The stock closed at C$0.32 on July 13, well below the new target.

news.headline: Paradigm Capital Names Perimeter a Healthcare Top Pick
news.summary: Paradigm Capital named Perimeter Medical Imaging AI a new speculative-buy Top Pick with a C$0.95 price target, pointing to the March 2026 FDA approval of Claire and noting the shares trade at roughly 2.3x 2027 revenue versus medtech M&A multiples averaging over 10x.

news.headline: Perimeter CEO Presents Corporate Update at Planet MicroCap Showcase in Las Vegas
news.summary: CEO Adrian Mendes presented a company update on Perimeter's commercial strategy for Claire OCT and discussed upcoming milestones at the Planet MicroCap Showcase in Las Vegas, following the participation announcement made on June 15, 2026.

news.headline: Perimeter Selected for INOVAIT Pilot Fund to Advance AI for Breast Cancer Surgery
news.summary: Perimeter was selected for a Government of Canada-backed INOVAIT Pilot Fund contribution of up to roughly C$148,000, plus a C$100,000 Mitacs award, to advance next-generation AI capabilities for its FDA-approved Claire breast-cancer margin assessment device.

news.headline: Perimeter Medical Imaging AI Closes Final Tranche of Non-Brokered Private Placement
news.summary: Perimeter closed the second and final CDN$3.28 million tranche of its convertible debenture private placement, bringing total proceeds to CDN$6.04 million of 3.59% debentures maturing April 2029, with major shareholder Social Capital and CEO Adrian Mendes participating.

news.headline: Perimeter Announces First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
news.summary: Perimeter reported Q1 2026 revenue of approximately $385,000 and disclosed the first two commercial placements of its newly FDA-approved Claire OCT+AI system at top-tier U.S. hospitals, with a sales funnel of nearly 50 qualified leads.

catalysts.label: CMS New Technology APC decision for Claire
catalysts.detail: Perimeter plans to file for a New Technology Ambulatory Payment Classification (APC) with CMS under the Medicare Outpatient Prospective Payment System, leveraging Claire's FDA Breakthrough Device status; a favorable decision would materially aid reimbursement and hospital adoption. CEO Adrian Mendes has said the company hopes to have reimbursement in place by early next year.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 financial results
catalysts.detail: Perimeter typically reports quarterly results within weeks of quarter-end (Q1 2026 was reported May 28, 2026); no official Q2 2026 date has yet been announced as of July 15, 2026.

catalysts.label: Nationwide commercial rollout of Claire OCT
catalysts.detail: Following March 2026 FDA PMA approval, Perimeter is converting its Claire sales funnel (nearly 50 qualified leads, several in advanced contracting stages) into additional commercial placements replacing legacy S-Series systems at U.S. hospitals.

catalysts.label: Potential Nasdaq uplisting
catalysts.detail: Management has publicly discussed a possible move from OTC/TSX-V to Nasdaq to broaden the capital markets pool for the stock, but no formal application or timeline has been confirmed.

pulse: Perimeter is in early commercial launch mode for Claire, its FDA-approved (March 2026) AI-enhanced OCT system for intraoperative breast-cancer margin assessment, having placed its first two Claire units at top-tier hospitals and reporting Q1 2026 revenue of about $385,000. The company has shored up its balance sheet via a CDN$6.04 million convertible debenture placement (closed June 2026), while a CMS New Technology APC reimbursement filing (decision expected by year-end 2026) is the key near-term catalyst. Sentiment has turned markedly more constructive, with Paradigm Capital (July 6) and Brookline Capital Markets (July 14) both initiating bullish coverage (targets C$0.95 and C$1.54 respectively) even as the stock trades around C$0.32, well below those targets.
